Oracle Events
- Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) for Terraform Digital, Sunbelt, Palmetto, vendor strategy, Heartland, Swift, Lakeline, margin, safeguard, stopgap, pricing-exposure, or attachment mismatch threads to find internal context on Elena's leadership decisions, Marcus vendor strategy memo inputs, David Chen stopgap requests, and pricing-exposure escalation framing.
- Search email (via search_emails) using queries related to Terraform Digital, Sunbelt, Palmetto, INV-2026-0398, INV-2026-0399, Raj Greenfield, Carmen Delgado-Reyes, Victoria Chen-Nakamura, Marcus Thorne, Samira Tariq, invoice PDF, vendor strategy, or reconstruction; use folder_name INBOX or SENT as needed to load client reconstruction mail, internal finance threads, and portfolio escalation.
- Search Linear (via linear_list_issues) with queries tied to Sunbelt, Palmetto, vendor strategy, CEO memo, Heartland, invoice attachment, Terraform, margin dataset, Emeka coverage, or safeguard to surface reconstruction, memo, safeguard, and related operations tickets.
- Open critical Linear issues (via linear_get_issue) using verified issue ids such as linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130 (Sunbelt/Palmetto PDF swap reconstruction), linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36 (CEO vendor strategy memo), linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (Q3 vendor margin dataset), linear_issue_3cd312558d78 (consolidated risk grid), linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 (invoice template safety checks), linear_issue_644c3b2e75a6 (Emeka coverage), or linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (Heartland Q1 dispute consolidation) as appropriate to the agent’s narrative.
- Load Linear thread comments (via linear_list_comments) for issueId linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130 and/or linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 to read Samira Tariq, David Chen, Marcus Thorne, and related engineering or finance notes on safeguards and reconstruction.
- Search CRM (via crm_search_companies and/or crm_list_engagements and/or crm_get_company) using name tokens Terraform, Vantage, Sunbelt, or Palmetto, and company_ids including company-terraform-digital where needed to retrieve engagement-terraform-replyall-incident (April 22, 2026 reply-all pricing leak to Rachel Nguyen, 25% margin floor, tiered discounts) and confirm Vantage Distributed naming on the Terraform company record.
- Search Airtable (via airtable_list_bases, then airtable_list_records, airtable_get_record, or airtable_search_records with the same base_id and table_name plus field_name and value as needed) using base_id appMoveOpsOps001 and table_name tblClientAccts01 or Client Accounts to read record recTerraformDigital01 and confirm Terraform Digital versus Vantage Distributed account context.
- Check calendar (via calendar_show_data and/or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to and/or calendar_search_events) for Elena Rostova or leadership prep; use start_datetime and end_datetime for the prep window the agent infers from the prompt for range queries, or query text for calendar_search_events. Expected: no conflicting prep events are found.
- Look up contacts (via contacts_search_contacts) with explicit query strings for Elena Rostova, Marcus Thorne, or Priya Chakrabarti (or similar) before send_email to confirm recipient and CC addresses.
- Send the executive briefing (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com, with subject and body covering Terraform reply-all leak, Sunbelt/Palmetto swapped PDF invoices and client escalation, vendor strategy memo and Heartland/Swift/Lakeline economics, engineering safeguard backlog, empty calendar, and Marcus review of numbers. The agent should also report a concise completion summary to the requester (Priya Chakrabarti) confirming the briefing was sent, Marcus was CC'd, the calendar was checked, and new Linear tickets were filed, without contradicting outbound client reconstruction visible in email tool results.
- Search QuickBooks (via quickbooks_search_invoices) for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices to verify INV-2026-0398 ($4,800, Sunbelt Venture Partners) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400, Palmetto Community Foundation, nonprofit rate), confirming that QuickBooks records are correct and only the PDF attachments were swapped in the outbound emails.
- Create untracked follow-up tickets (via linear_create_issue, typically three calls) on team Operations or equivalent with titles aligned to recruit/onboard a dedicated Terraform relocation coordinator, leadership decision on temporary 4–6 week coordinator stopgap, and Catalina Dubois Midwest enterprise exposure inputs for the CEO vendor strategy memo

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 A Linear issue was created (via linear_create_issue) whose title contains wording substantially equivalent to recruiting or onboarding a dedicated Terraform relocation coordinator for Terraform Digital.
outcome

None mention recruiting, onboarding, or hiring a dedicated Terraform relocation coordinator.
✗Run #4Only three linear_create_issue calls were made: (1) corrected invoices for Sunbelt/Palmetto, (2) Catalina's Midwest account exposure, (3) re-prioritize invoice attachment safeguard. None of these relate to recruiting or onboarding a dedicated Terraform relocation coordinator.
✗Run #5Only three linear_create_issue calls were made: corrected invoices for Sunbelt/Palmetto, Catalina's exposure notes, and the engineering safeguard escalation. None mention recruiting or onboarding a dedicated Terraform relocation coordinator.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #4The email body contains 'Marcus — please flag anything on the numbers that needs correcting before Elena uses this.' This explicitly asks Marcus to validate/correct the numbers before Elena uses the briefing.

If any of those have landed since the tickets were last updated, please flag so Elena has the latest.' This explicitly asks Marcus to validate/correct vendor memo timing and numbers.
7 The email to "elena.rostova@moveops.com" states that on 2026-04-22 Catalina Dubois caused a reply-all or reply all internal pricing leak to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital or Vantage Distributed.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The email body describes the reply-all pricing leak to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital but does not mention the specific date 2026-04-22 (or April 22) and does not explicitly name Catalina Dubois as the one who caused the reply-all. It says 'accidentally forwarded to Rachel Nguyen via reply-all' without identifying Catalina or the date.
✗Run #2The email states 'On ~April 21, Catalina accidentally reply-all'd an internal pricing strategy email to Rachel Nguyen (VP People Ops, Terraform Digital).' The date is April 21 (with ~), not April 22 as required. The criteria specifically requires April 22 or 2026-04-22.
✗Run #3The email describes the Terraform pricing leak as 'accidentally forwarded' but does not mention the date 2026-04-22, does not name Catalina Dubois as the person who caused the leak, and does not describe the mechanism as a 'reply-all'. The CRM record (engagement-terraform-replyall-incident) was not found because crm_search_companies returned 0 results for 'Terraform Digital'.
✗Run #4The email does not state that Catalina Dubois caused a reply-all leak on 2026-04-22. It attributes the Terraform pricing leak to Alejandro's thread being 'accidentally forwarded' to Rachel Nguyen, with no specific date of April 22 and no mention of Catalina as the sender.
✗Run #5The email does not mention Catalina Dubois as the person who caused the leak, does not specify a 2026-04-22 date, and does not describe a 'reply-all' mechanism. It only says internal pricing 'was accidentally forwarded' to Rachel Nguyen, with no details about who triggered it or when.
✗Run #6The email mentions Catalina Dubois, reply-all, Rachel Nguyen, and Terraform Digital together, but does not include the specific date April 22 or 2026-04-22 for the incident.
8 The email to "elena.rostova@moveops.com" states that a 25% margin floor was exposed to the client.
outcome
 ✗✓✓✓✓✓ 83%
✗Run #1The email says 'The chain included Alejandro's full margin modeling (cost basis, margin floors, tier breakpoints at 8%/12% discount)' but never explicitly states the specific 25% margin floor figure. The 25% non-negotiable margin floor is not mentioned in the email body.
✓Run #2The email states 'our 25% margin floor' in direct reference to what was exposed to the client via the reply-all.
✓Run #3The email body states 'Rachel now knows our 25% margin floor', clearly attributing this as exposed to the client.
✓Run #4The email body states 'She now knows our 25% margin floor', directly referencing the 25% margin floor exposure to the client.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly states 'our full pricing strategy (25% margin floor, tiered discount structure at 8%/12%...)' in the Terraform section. The 25% margin floor is clearly stated in the context of client exposure.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly states: 'Rachel now has visibility into our 25% margin floor', satisfying the requirement to state the 25% margin floor was exposed to the client.
9 The email to "elena.rostova@moveops.com" states that tiered volume discounts, including 8% at 11-20 moves and 12% at 21+ moves, were exposed.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body states: 'Rachel has seen the tiered discount structure (Tier 1: standard, Tier 2: 8% at 11-20 moves/quarter, Tier 3: 12% at 21+).' Both the 8% at 11-20 moves and 12% at 21+ thresholds are explicitly stated.
✓Run #2The email states 'the tiered volume discount structure (8% at 11–20 moves/quarter, 12% at 21+)' — both tiers with the correct thresholds are present.

No evidence in the trajectory of Emeka sending a reconstruction/explanation email to Raj, so the agent's framing is consistent with available tool-visible evidence and does not make the specific denied-explanation claim the rubric targets.
